TextENGNew: ......I must have drifted off at some point despite everything.
I woke up to Mion poking me......
"Time to get up."
"Mmn...... Oh, yeah...... You actually woke up on time without any alarm. Impressive......"
"Mii-chan didn't sleep at all."
"You're the one who snored the loudest of us all. Quite a set of nerves on you, Keiichi-san."
changeReason: Old says 'slept soundest'; new says 'snored the loudest.' JPN グースカ寝てた specifically implies snoring/sleeping noisily, not just sleeping deeply

TextENGNew: "......It doesn't feel, pitch dark......"
changeReason: Old says 'It should be pitch black, but it isn't' (assertion about expectation); new says 'It doesn't feel pitch dark' (subjective impression). JPN という感じはしない means 'doesn't feel like,' new matches the uncertainty in the source

TextENGNew: "True. Moonlight is surprisingly bright."
If it really had been only moonlight, it might have been a little romantic.
Traffic lights and other illumination that had been left on kept lighting the city without rest.
Unlike Hinamizawa, Okinomiya never fell into absolute darkness at night.
I couldn't tell whether that was fortunate or a disadvantage for our stealth.
From time to time, flashlight beams flickered over the main road — a river of unattended cars.
They had set a watch, just as we expected.
Still, their alertness was far thinner than it was during the day.
"......Okay. We can do this......"
"......We're going to, be okay, aren't we......?"
"Leave it to us. There's nothing to be afraid of. We'll cross it easily."
"We've got our secret weapon too."
"I'd really like to do this quietly. If we come barreling in making noise, we'll cause problems for the people in the office too."
"Fair enough."
From the reconnaissance, the Sonozaki office appeared to have cut off all contact with the outside.
They had fortified themselves with barricades and had no apparent interaction with the rioters.
If they had surrendered their hearts to the demon like the rioters, they wouldn't have been fortifying themselves — they'd have been out joining the hunt for the infected.
But if we came crashing in with rioters in pursuit, the Sonozaki Group would be accused of sheltering the infected, sparking another conflict.
Complete stealth was the only way.
Une slowly unlocked the door that led outside.
In Okinomiya, where the streets were as good as an ocean teeming with sharks, opening the front door felt like stepping into a lion's cage......
"It's not like, I'm not scared......"
"I'll take point. ......If I fail, fall back quickly as planned. Don't even think about coming back for me. Understood!"
".........That goes both ways. If I twist my ankle this time, leave me behind."
"We really will this time, so tread carefully, Keiichi-kun."
"Got it!"
"Systems confirmed. Leave it to me when the time comes."
Satoko was carrying a remote-controlled car borrowed from a second floor somewhere.
She had tested it driving in a straight line with the remote, with an empty can tied behind it to drag along.
It could serve as a decoy in a crisis. The only downside was that the noise would draw unwanted attention.
It wasn't a trump card to reach the office — it was a trump card to buy time for a retreat if the operation failed.
Mion estimated the operation's success rate at 95%. But that still left a chance of failure above zero.
Following her policy of anticipating every possible scenario, Mion gave Satoko's remote-control decoy plan the green light just in case.
"Moving out. ......Keep a fair distance between yourselves and me."
She eased the door open, took a long moment to feel out the silence around her...... and slipped outside.
......Unlike Hinamizawa, Okinomiya was covered in concrete, and even the smallest sounds echoed unexpectedly.
Step on a small pebble on the asphalt, and the sound carried remarkably far.
"......Actually...... going barefoot might be better......"
Mion took off her shoes.
No matter how carefully you walked, the hard rubber soles couldn't silence the sound of grit grinding against asphalt.
Even with socks, a city kid would find walking barefoot on pavement painful.
But for us Hinamizawa kids who ran around and climbed trees barefoot like it was nothing, walking on asphalt without shoes was trivial.
"I wonder if our former city boy Keiichi-san can handle the barefoot treatment. Worth watching."
"I've been toughened up plenty since then. Don't sell me short."
"Are you okay going barefoot, Une-chan......?"
"Sorry. Just bear with it for a little while, okay......"
Mion poked just her head out of the alley and checked on the main road — the river of unattended cars.
The wide sidewalk gave too clear a sightline. The only option was to dash across in one go and take cover among the cars.
The self-styled watch, too, clearly understood that clear view made it the perfect lookout point.
Every so often, a flashlight beam swept straight down the sidewalk.
......They were excessively frightened, looking for an enemy.
Even the faintest presence would send them shouting and summoning the others.
".........Now this is good. This is the kind of thrill I've been after."
Mion licked the sweat running down her nose with a bold grin.
She signaled her friends with hand signs to hold, then watched for her moment.
......The watch's lights were restless.
They wandered back and forth, looking in every direction nervously.
Their fear drove them to light up every shadow.
That very restlessness created plenty of openings......
Swift and nimble as a cat. Mion sprinted across the sidewalk and dove into the row of cars.
It was over so quickly and cleanly that all the tension beforehand seemed absurd.
Keiichi and the others watching from the shadows let out a deep breath of relief.
Keiichi, as second in line, was about to follow, but Mion sent the sign to hold.
She seemed to want to carefully work her way through the line of cars and check the sidewalk on the far side.
When she'd scouted it from the rooftop right before the operation, there had been no one over there.
But at some point, three figures from the watch had appeared on the sidewalk on the far side.
......And they were heading right toward her.
It was all right — she hadn't been spotted. They were just strolling around shining lights randomly at the cars.
Mion calmly spotted a truck with enough clearance and slid underneath it......
It was fine, it was fine...... She hadn't been spotted.
If they'd seen her, would they really be walking around so carelessly......?
Even knowing she was 99% safe...... just thinking about the remaining 1% made her feel a cold sweat coming on.
changeReason: New adds 'the remaining 1%' which makes the 99% context explicit; old only mentions sweating without specifying why. JPN 絶対ではないこと (the fact that it's not absolute) is made concrete in new

TextENGNew: ......Pathetic, Mion Sonozaki. To think I'd be sweating over something as basic as hide-and-seek......
"Make sure you don't let any infected slip through......"
"They've got damaged brains. And they'll try to bite us to spread the virus."
If anyone's brain is damaged, it's yours. Mion smirked bitterly.
But her smile froze when their feet stopped right in front of the truck she was hiding under.
"D-Did they find her...?!"
"......We're, we're done for........."
"Calm down. ......Something's off about how they're acting."
"Are they climbing into the truck bed?"
The men from the watch climbed up into the truck bed that Mion was hiding beneath.
Apparently there were folding chairs or something stored in the truck bed. The men sat down.
In other words, the men had positioned themselves directly above Mion. She was like an insect pinned to a board......
"Wh...... Seriously......?"
Cold sweat dripped from Mion's forehead.
Keiichi and the others understood the situation. Mion couldn't move an inch.
There was no guarantee they wouldn't hear her crawl out from under the truck. On top of that, Mion had no way to see what was happening above her.
Even if she somehow slipped out without a sound, there was no guarantee they wouldn't happen to glance over and spot her.
......Mion couldn't move until those men had left......
"I-It's not like, I know what to do......"
"Is it finally time for the secret weapon?!"
"Only as a last resort. It could get Mion out, but it'll make a terrible ruckus...!"
"Still, get it ready."
Satoko nodded, set the remote-controlled car on the sidewalk, and tied the empty can to it.
Once it was set loose, the racket it made would definitely draw their attention.
"Either way, the operation's blown. We need to create an opening for Mion to escape. Better to try again from scratch."
"......Agreed. We just had bad timing. Let's start over."
Having been through so many dangerous club activities, the members knew precisely when to pull back.
Mion was thinking the same thing, naturally. Keiichi and the others would create a chance for her to escape. She had to slip out in that gap.
......But not yet.
There was still a chance the men in the truck bed would stand up and move off somewhere.
Even a successful retreat, if it created an uproar, would make a second crossing far more difficult.
Even with her heart essentially pinned in place, Mion kept calmly watching for her chance at victory......
"Get Une-chan back to safety first."
She wasn't used to going barefoot. If it came down to a sprint, she might not be able to give it her all.
"Keiichi-kun, take Une-chan and secure our retreat. Satoko-chan, guard my back."
"Leave it to me. You've got a big responsibility, Keiichi-san!"
"Understood. We're counting on you. Let's go, Une-chan...!"
changeReason: Old says 'I'll leave this to you two'; new says 'We're counting on you.' JPN ここは任すぜ does not specify a number; old added 'two' not present in source

TextENGNew: Keiichi took Une and withdrew for the time being.
......I thought I'd faced death in Hinamizawa. But it was mostly old people there. And we'd had the home field advantage.
Here was a completely different game — pure away territory.
"If I'd known it was going to come to this, I'd have spent more time exploring Okinomiya......"
"Hahh, hahh, hahh......"
Une was gasping for breath.
No surprise there. Being wound this tight took it out of you. Fear alone was enough to make you breathless.
changeReason: Old says 'No one could blame her' and refers only to her exhaustion; new generalizes to 'you' and says 'Fear alone was enough to make you breathless.' JPN addresses fear alongside exhaustion differently, but the shift from her-specific to general 'you' is notable

TextENGNew: "Let's stop for a moment and catch our breath."
Once Une's breathing had steadied, Keiichi sharpened all his senses and swept the area for any presence.
......He found one almost immediately.
"This is bad...... Someone's coming."
"Multiple sets of footsteps. The watch. ....................."
There was almost nowhere to hide. Getting spotted now would cut off Mion's escape route too. Any risk was out of the question.
There were no other streets. They had to turn back and pray the men went elsewhere.
But the main road seemed to be the artery of their patrol circuit. There was a high chance they were heading straight toward Rena and the others.
"Head back. We can't run into them here."
"W-We're going back, again......? A-A pincer move......"
"Might be. Anyway, back we go...!"
Keiichi's instinct was right. The men didn't turn down any other alley — they were heading straight for the main road.
Three young men around college age, carrying bent metal bats.
changeReason: Old says 'four young men'; new says 'three.' JPN ３人 means three; old had the wrong number

TextENGNew: At this rate they'd hit Rena and the others from behind.
"A pincer? That's bad......"
"Now this is interesting. I didn't think we'd get suspense on par with club activities."
"No way back. So what do we do?"
"Y-You don't mean...... do you......?"
"If we can't go back, then we go forward!"
"......We'll have to use the remote-control trap to draw the enemy's eyes...... and push forward in that gap."
A dangerous gambit. It would probably get us through this particular pinch.
But the noise would wake the rioters resting nearby, all at once.
If they had the bad luck to run headlong into those rioters, we'd be trapped like rats in a bag.
Two or three opponents — with all the club members together, we might manage.
But if a huge mob came running in response to the noise, we'd just be kids with no way to fight back.
Still, even stacked with misfortunes, this was not a game with zero chance of winning.
"Satoko-chan, trap ready......"
"Whenever you say."
"Sidewalk is clear. We can cross now...!"
"Go! Satoko-chan, now!"
"Leave it to me!"
"Move, Une-chan...! Run!"
"It's not like, I can't run!"
We all crossed at once and dove into the line of cars.
But there was no preventing the men in the truck above from noticing our dash.
The moment they looked to see what it was, Satoko's remote-control trap activated.
It dragged the empty can behind it, making a harsh metallic clatter as it raced down the sidewalk.
"W-What?! What's that sound?!"
"They're running! After them!! Has to be the infected!!"
Their paranoia and fear transformed into violent aggression.
The men leapt from the truck bed onto car roofs and hoods, charging after the sound of the remote-controlled car.
The midnight silence shattered, shouts and whistle blasts erupting from all directions. The city's madness came fully awake.
But Mion, pinned under the truck, was finally free.
"What are you doing?! It was still too early!"
"We were pincered! We had no choice!"
"Une-san, now is the time to show what you're made of!"
"Hahh, hahh, hahh...!!"
"There's no going back! More are coming that way!"
Rioters with bloodshot eyes came pouring one after another from the very alley everyone had just been hiding in.
There must have been a building or something housing them nearby.
The option of retreating to safety was cut off. Forward was the only way.
"If we have to go forward, then forward it is! We're almost there!"
"Let's pray the Sonozaki Group hasn't forgotten your face."
"Hahaha, Kei-chan, you do say the funniest things!"
"Don't let your guard down! We can't relax until we're inside the Sonozaki Group office!"
"Agreed. There's still a nonzero chance Shion-san greets us with a 'Who's your sister? Blehhh!'"
"Right. More importantly, Mion — you haven't forgotten your promise about the belly dance, have you? You made it when Une-chan threw that ladder down for us!"
"Ihihi, did this old man say something like that?"
"Hahh, hahh, hahh...!!"
"Une-chan, hang in there! Just a bit further!"
"Just around that corner! ...... Huh?!"
changeReason: Old says 'It's right around that corner!'; while the text is similar, old preserved the surprise reaction whereas new restructures the line differently with removed voice tag mid-line

TextENGNew: I think Mion had completely let her guard down.
All that was left was the final sprint to the finish and cutting the tape.
But the finish line was not something that could simply be cut through.
"What—?! W-What is this?!"
"W-What the—?!"
The alley was sealed off with barbed wire mesh.
Whether it was the Sonozaki Group securing safety in front of their office, or the rioters keeping the infected from getting through, there was no way to know.
But the fact was that the alley was blocked off, with the office right in front of us.
Had we known about this barricade in advance, our plan would have been entirely different.
It was nothing but bad luck. That one spot alone was in the perfect blind spot from the angle Mion had been surveying through the opera glasses.
And a barricade had been built in that very blind spot — a double misfortune!
It wasn't impossible to climb over it if you were willing to tear yourself up on the barbed wire.
But we couldn't demand that of Une, who didn't share the resolve of us club members.
"Alternate routes?!"
"Th-there is one! If we swing around from the alley on the other side...!!"
That was when blazing flashlight beams caught us all at once.
"Who are you?!!"
"Hyahahahaha! There! It's the infected!!"
Our blood ran cold. It was over.
Unable to endure the terror any longer, Une let out a piercing scream......
There was nothing we could do. Our opponents had us beaten in size, ferocity, and numbers.
The rioters swarmed in from all sides...... surrounding us in the dead end formed by the barricade......
That was when several dry gunshots rang out in rapid succession.
Mion immediately shouted "Down!" and pushed everyone's heads to the ground.
The rioters, unused to the sound of gunfire, stood there blinking.
"Gentlemen, that's enough! Mob punishment without legal basis is unacceptable under any circumstances!!"
It was the sound of Tomitake firing into the air with a submachine gun he'd taken from the Irie Institution's security room.
Perhaps the rioters thought it was a model gun or something — they showed no fear, only glaring at Tomitake for interrupting their hunt.
With Okinomiya's medical facilities completely paralyzed, even a shot to the leg could prove fatal.
Tomitake had no wish to shoot, and he swallowed hard as the rioters showed no sign of backing down......
"I don't want to shoot, everyone...!"
"Leave this to me. ......Hanyuu!"
"Brethren of mine...... Calm your spirits...... I am Oyashiro-sama, your leader...!"
Hanyuu's solemn words rang out for inhuman ears alone.
The rioters stood there slack-jawed, blinking as if half asleep.
One by one they sat down right on the ground and stared into space.
Ferocious as Takano had made them, they were still Hanyuu's kin. They could not escape the power of her voice.
Mion and the others stared in disbelief, but they understood — they had escaped death by the narrowest margin.
"Everyone, now while you can......!"
"W-What is this...? What's going on?!"
"Oyashiro Power, nipah~☆"
"Thank you for saving us, Rika-chan! Tomitake-san!"
"I heard the commotion. Good thing we rushed over. What were you all doing out here?"
"Mion-san's Sonozaki Group office is just right beyond here."
changeReason: Old says 'The office of the Sonozaki Group is just beyond here'; new adds 'Mion-san's Sonozaki Group office.' JPN explicitly mentions 魅音さんの (Mion-san's), which old omitted

TextENGNew: "We didn't know about the barricade and got cornered like rats."
"How embarrassing. You cut it that close with my sis on your side?"
"Shion!"
Beyond the barricade were Shion, Kasai, and a few men in black.
They all carried guns, but none of them wore the ferocious expressions of the rioters.
It seemed the Sonozaki Group had kept their sanity and focused on holding out inside.
"Give us a break. We really don't want to get tangled up with those lunatics."
"Sorry for causing such a commotion."
"If you're safe, Shion-san, then there's nothing to be afraid of anymore."
"That's right. Alone I'm worth about ten of my sister. Kasai, let them in."
"Come in, everyone."
Kasai undid the padlock on the chains that had been securely fastened to the barricade gate.
"But what on earth happened here? What did you do to them?"
"Just what, on earth......"
"Don't worry about it. Leave them alone and they'll wander off on their own."
"Now, now! Let's be grateful for this chance granted to us by Oyashiro-sama's miraculous power, hahaha!"
"Come on, Une-chan, you're safe now!"
"With Shion-san here too, there's absolutely nothing to worry about!"
"That's right. At least Satoko gets it. ......By the way, who's this girl?"
"This is Une-chan. She saved our lives!"
"......What's wrong, Hanyuu?"
Everyone slipped through the barricade and was welcomed into the Sonozaki Group office.
That sight...... was being observed from a distance through binoculars......
".........Hehe, hehe. ......Found you. ......So that's the one......"
